ports_extended: GmailAdapter.getAllLabels (relocated from legacy GmailLabelService — cache + system labels + return-[] on error)

enforcement.invariant_doc: src/CLAUDE.md (hexagonal invariant, approved port list, seam pattern, approved fallback)
enforcement.ci_guard: tests-local/architecture-boundary.test.js (greps src/features/** for forbidden SDK tokens; verified it catches planted violations)
enforcement.cookie_strict: cookie SKILL.md updated out-of-repo: Step 5b HARD BLOCK rule + BLOCK criteria + Proof of Work entry

dod_verification: grep of src/features/** for GmailApp|SpreadsheetApp|DriveApp|UrlFetchApp|PropertiesService|CacheService|Utilities.|callGeminiApi|GmailService = CLEAN (only UnifiedCacheService app-service + comments remain). Full jest suite green. clasp push: up to date.
summary: Routed ALL feature code through src/core/services ports. Created GeminiAdapter/PropertiesAdapter/UtilitiesAdapter and consolidated Gmail access onto GmailAdapter (legacy GmailService no longer referenced in features). Behavior identical (structural refactor, no logic changes). Enforcement added: invariant doc, CI guard test with teeth, and strict Cookie rubric. Deployed.
backlog_items: OPTION: convert email HTML -> Markdown before Gemini (carried over from prior leg).

OPTIONAL: GmailAdapter exposes a few methods (sendEmail/getUserEmailAddress/batchProcessThreads) that reach MailApp/Session/Utilities internally — these live in the core/services boundary so they are allowed, but could be made injectable for fuller DI if desired.
OPTIONAL: legacy src/core/gmail-service.js (GmailLabelService) is still used by non-feature core callers; consider deprecating fully once those migrate.
issues: Environment had no Agent/Task sub-agent dispatch tool available, so the orchestrator implemented the waves directly while still gating each wave through Oscar reviewers (ernie/cookie) per the rubric. Oscar artifacts were produced inline rather than as separate sub-agent threads.
